name: Chicken Szechuan-Style with Sesame Paste
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 tablespoons Dry sherry or Shaoshing wine
recipeInstructions:
1. In a pot, poach the chicken breasts in a little boiling water for 10 minutes until white and opaque.
2. Toast the Szechuan peppercorns over moderate heat, then crush them; set aside.
3. Remove the chicken breasts from the pot, drain, and cool them. Slice them, then shred into julienned pieces.
4. In a mixing bowl, combine the sesame paste (or peanut butter) with green tea (or omit). Add the vinegar and soy sauce; blend well.
5. Add the peanut oil, crushed red pepper, ginger, scallion, garlic, sherry, cayenne pepper, and peppercorns to the mixture. Mix all ingredients together thoroughly.
6. Toss the chicken strips in this sauce to coat them evenly.
7. Refrigerate for at least 20 minutes before serving.
8. Serve the chicken with lettuce leaves; let each guest place a small portion of the chicken on a leaf and roll it up like an egg roll to eat using their fingers.
9. Makes 6 appetizer servings.
NOTE: The authentic recipe calls for green tea to thin the sesame seed paste, but you may substitute chicken broth or water if preferred. An alternative is to use peanut butter with sesame oil instead of sesame paste. This dish can be served cold or at room temperature and can be prepared up to one day in advance.
